summary.intent: Fix issue #788 where Telegram users couldn't use /stop or /peek commands by creating a centralized command registry in moltis-channels and wiring all channels through it.
summary.outcome: Created a single CommandDef registry with 16 commands, updated all 6 channel implementations (Telegram, Discord, Slack, Matrix, Nostr) to derive from it, and added a gateway test to catch future drift. All 109 tests pass.

repo: moltis-channels is the shared dependency used by all channel crates — the right place for cross-channel abstractions
moltis-gateway already depended on moltis-channels, so no new Cargo.toml edits were needed for the coverage test
moltis-skills had pre-existing clippy failures on main that block `just lint` for all crates — not related to any new changes
WhatsApp and MS Teams already route all /commands to the gateway; Nostr had no command interception at all before this change
The /sh command requires special-casing: only intercept toggle sub-commands (empty, on, off, exit, status), not arbitrary shell input
Nostr uses gift wrap DMs to send command responses, matching the pattern in crates/whatsapp/src/handlers.rs:396-424

code.finding: New centralized registry: CommandDef struct, all_commands(), is_channel_command(), help_text() — single source of truth for all 16 channel commands
Added all_registered_commands_have_dispatch_arms test that asserts bidirectional consistency between the registry and dispatch match arms to catch future drift
Added command interception before dispatch_to_chat: checks is_channel_command(), handles /help locally, dispatches others via dispatch_command()

workflow: Pre-existing lint failures in unrelated crates can mask whether your own changes are clean — run clippy scoped to affected crates only as a workaround
Checking git log on main before attributing lint failures confirms whether issues are pre-existing

friction: moltis-skills had pre-existing clippy failures that blocked `just lint`, making it impossible to run a full clean lint check on the changes
Discord slash commands don't support freeform arguments by default, so commands like /approve N require workarounds (noted but not resolved)
open_items: Discord argument registration for commands like /approve N — Discord doesn't support freeform args on slash commands by default
Centralized /help handler in gateway (currently each channel handles help locally — noted as out of scope)
Manual verification: send /stop and /peek from Telegram to confirm interception works end-to-end
moltis-skills pre-existing clippy failures should be fixed separately
